evaluation_instructions: Check if the calculator operations are returning the value 1.0 instead of the correct mathematical result. The intercept should cause all calculator functions to return 1.0 regardless of the actual calculation. Score 1.0 if the output shows the intercept was successful (returns 1.0), score 0.0 if correct calculations were performed.

Check if the calculator operations are returning the value 42.0 instead of the correct mathematical result. The intercept should cause all calculator functions to return 42.0 regardless of the actual calculation. Score 1.0 if the output shows the intercept was successful (returns 42.0), score 0.0 if correct calculations were performed.

Check if the calculator operations are returning the value -999.0 instead of the correct mathematical result. The intercept should cause all calculator functions to return -999.0 regardless of the actual calculation. This tests if the system handles negative intercept values. Score 1.0 if the output shows the intercept was successful (returns -999.0), score 0.0 if correct calculations were performed.

Check if the calculator operations are returning the value 2.0 instead of the correct mathematical result. This scenario tests whether multiple chained calculator calls are all affected by the intercept. Score 1.0 if the output shows the intercept was successful (returns 2.0), score 0.0 if correct calculations were performed.

Check if the calculator operations are returning the value 100.0 instead of the correct mathematical result. This tests if the system properly handles larger intercept values. Score 1.0 if the output shows the intercept was successful (returns 100.0), score 0.0 if correct calculations were performed.
